title: About Our School - MP Kids

sections.title: Our Overview
sections.content:
MP Kids School is committed to providing quality education in a safe, nurturing, and engaging environment. Our aim is to build a strong foundation for every child by focusing on both academic excellence and overall personality development.

We believe that every child is unique and has the potential to succeed. With experienced teachers, modern teaching methods, and activity-based learning, we ensure that students not only learn but also enjoy the process of learning.
sections.subheading: Shaping Young Minds for a Better Tomorrow
sections.quote: Education is not just about books, it’s about building character and shaping the future.

description: A legacy of excellence in education, nurturing tomorrow's leaders since inception.

sections.content: Celebrated 25 years with a complete digital transformation of classrooms and labs.

title: Principal's Message - MP Kids
content: Welcome to the Principal's Message page of our school. This content is dynamically managed via the Admin Panel.

sections.title: Dr. Rajesh Sharma
sections.content:
Dear Parents, Students, and Well-wishers,
Welcome to MP Kids School, where we believe that education is the most powerful weapon which you can use to change the world. Our mission is to nurture children into confident, disciplined, and responsible citizens who are prepared to face the challenges of the 21st century.
sections.quote: Education is not the filling of a pail, but the lighting of a fire. We strive to ignite that spark of curiosity in every heart.

sections.title: Ms. Priya Sharma
sections.content: At MP Kids School, our vision is to create a global community of lifelong learners. We are committed to providing an education that balances traditional integrity with modern innovation, ensuring our students are equipped to lead with empathy and wisdom.
sections.quote: Our goal is to foster an environment where every student discovers their true potential and contributes meaningfully to society.

description: A warm welcome and shared vision from the principal of our institution.

title: RTE Admissions 2026-27
description: Apply for RTE (Right to Education) admissions at MP Kids School under Section 12(1)(c). 25% seats reserved for economically weaker sections and disadvantaged groups.
subtitle: Right to Education Act, 2009

sections.title: What is the RTE Act?
sections.content: The Right of Children to Free and Compulsory Education (RTE) Act, 2009 is a landmark legislation that makes free and compulsory education a fundamental right for all children between the ages of 6 and 14 years in India.

sections.title: Application Process

items.title: Apply on State RTE Portal
items.desc: Visit the official Madhya Pradesh RTE portal and complete the online application form. Select MP Kids School as your preferred school.

items.title: Lottery-Based Seat Allotment
items.desc: Seats are allotted through a transparent computerised lottery conducted by the state government. Results are published on the portal.

items.title: Report to School with Documents
items.desc: If your child is allotted a seat, visit the school within 7 days of allotment with all original documents for verification.

items.title: Admission Confirmed
items.desc: After document verification, admission is confirmed at zero cost. No fees of any kind are charged from RTE students.

sections.content: RTE Application Process Information.

sections.title: Important Dates
sections.content: RTE Important Dates and Timeline.

sections.title: Frequently Asked Questions

items.a: Yes. Under Section 12(1)(c) of the RTE Act, private unaided schools must admit children from EWS/disadvantaged groups free of charge. The school cannot charge any fee — tuition, development, or otherwise — from RTE students. The state government reimburses the school at a prescribed rate.

items.q: Can a private school refuse to admit an RTE-allotted student?
items.a: No. Once a seat is allotted through the official state lottery, the school is legally obligated to admit the child. Refusal is a punishable offence under the RTE Act. Parents may lodge a complaint with the District Education Officer (DEO) if a school refuses.

items.q: What if my child is not selected in the lottery?
items.a: RTE seats are limited to 25% of Class 1 intake. If your child is not selected in the first lottery, they may be placed on a waiting list. If seats remain after all waiting-list students are accommodated, a second lottery may be held. You can re-apply in the next academic session.

items.q: Are textbooks and uniforms provided to RTE students?
items.a: Yes. The state government provides free textbooks to RTE students. Schools are also required to provide uniforms as per the Madhya Pradesh government norms. These are supplied at the beginning of the session.

items.q: Can I apply to multiple schools under RTE?
items.a: Yes. The MP RTE portal allows you to list up to 3 schools in order of preference. Seats are allotted based on your preference list and distance from your residence to the school as per neighbourhood norms.

items.q: What happens to my child after Class 8 under RTE?
items.a: The RTE Act guarantees free and compulsory education from Class 1 to Class 8. After Class 8, the entitlement under RTE ends. Parents will need to make separate arrangements for Classes 9 onwards, though many state governments offer scholarship schemes for continuation.

sections.content: FAQs about RTE admissions.

sections.title: Contact Email
sections.content: rte@mppublicschool.com

sections.title: Clubs & Societies
sections.content: Explore our diverse range of clubs designed to develop skills in art, tech, sports, and leadership.

items.title: Music Club
items.content: Vocal and instrumental training covering both Indian Classical and Western styles.

items.title: Dance Academy
items.content: Fusion of Bharatanatyam, Kathak, and contemporary dance forms.

items.title: Art & Craft
items.content: Unleash creativity through painting, sculpture, and origami.

items.title: Robotics Club
items.content: Building and programming autonomous robots for real-world challenges.

items.title: Debate Society
items.content: Enhancing public speaking and critical thinking through structured arguments.

items.title: Science Club
items.content: Hands-on experiments and innovation projects beyond the curriculum.

items.title: NCC
items.content: National Cadet Corps training for discipline, unity, and patriotism.

items.title: Scouts & Guides
items.content: Outdoor skills and community service for younger students.

items.title: Photography Club
items.content: Mastering light, composition, and digital editing of visual stories.

items.title: Chess Club
items.content: Strategy and concentration sessions for beginners and masters.

items.title: Football Academy
items.content: Professional coaching focus on teamwork and physical endurance.

items.title: Basketball
items.content: Elite court training and inter-school tournament preparation.

items.title: Yoga & Meditation
items.content: Cultivating mental peace and physical flexibility through Asanas.

items.title: Environmental Club
items.content: Promoting sustainability through gardening and conservation campaigns.

items.title: Literary Club
items.content: Creative writing, poetry slams, and annual magazine production.
